items.hook: Cheshire competed in the 2013 FIS Freestyle Skiing World Cup in Voss Municipality, Norway, where she finished 17th.
items.body: Rowan Cheshire is a British freestyle skier, specialising in the halfpipe. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Milwaukee Cup is a traveling trophy awarded to the winner of the annual men's soccer match between Marquette University and the University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee.

items.hook: Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa: After Pope Innocent III calls European knights to a crusade, the forces of kings Alfonso VIII of Castile, Sancho VII of Navarre, Peter II of Aragon and Afonso II of Portugal defeat those of the Berber Muslim leader Almohad, thus marking a significant turning point in the Reconquista and in the medieval history of Spain.
items.body: The Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa, known in Islamic history as the Battle of Al-Uqab, took place on 16 July 1212 and was an important turning point in the Reconquista and the history of medieval Spain. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Collaborative pedagogy stems from the process theory of rhetoric and composition.
items.body: Collaborative pedagogy believes that students will better engage with writing, critical thinking, and revision if they engage with others. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: State Route 138 is an east–west state highway in the U.S.
items.body: state of California that generally follows the northern foothills of the San Gabriel Mountains and the western Mojave Desert. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: They were produced by the Piccard-Pictet Company until 1920, and by Gnome et Rhône from 1920 until the demise of the marque in 1924.
items.body: Pic-Pic was a Swiss automobile manufactured in Geneva from 1906 to 1924. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Parliament of the Ukrainian SSR declares state sovereignty over the territory of the Ukrainian SSR.
items.body: The Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine is the unicameral parliament of Ukraine. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The M1 road is a road in northern Zambia.
items.body: It connects Mpika in Muchinga Province with Mbala in the Northern Province on the border with Tanzania and the Lake Tanganyika port of Mpulungu via Kasama. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Henry Huttleston Rogers was an American industrialist and financier.
items.body: He made his fortune in the oil refining business, becoming a leader at Standard Oil. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: King Leopold III of Belgium abdicates in favor of his son, Baudouin of Belgium.
items.body: Leopold III was King of the Belgians from 23 February 1934 until his abdication on 16 July 1951. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Meißen area, where Radebeul is located, is one of the northeasternmost areas where wine is grown in the 21st century.
items.body: Radebeul is a town in the Elbe valley in the district of Meißen in Saxony, Germany, a suburb of Dresden. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Zhargalma Ochirovna Tsyrenova is a Russian freestyle wrestler of Buryat descent.
items.body: She is 2013 European wrestling championships runner up and a bronze medalist at the Golden Grand Prix Ivan Yarygin 2015 in the 60 kg division. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: As of the 2010 Census, its population was 5,145.
items.body: Apastovo is an urban locality and the administrative center of Apastovsky District of the Republic of Tatarstan, Russia, located 109 kilometers (68 mi) from the republic's capital of Kazan. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Sharad Anantrao Joshi was an Indian politician, who founded the Swatantra Bharat Paksh party and Shetkari Sanghatana.
items.body: He was also a Member of the Parliament of India representing Maharashtra in the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of the Indian Parliament from 5 July 2004 to 4 July 2010. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Manchu Qing dynasty naval forces under commander Shi Lang defeat the Kingdom of Tungning in the Battle of Penghu near the Pescadores Islands.
items.body: The Qing dynasty, officially the Great Qing, also known as the Qing Empire or Qing China, was a Manchu-led imperial dynasty of China and an early modern empire in East Asia which existed from 1636/1644 to 1912. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The 100 best novels of all time
items.body: The top 100 novels of all time published in English, as voted for by authors, critics and academics worldwide. How many have you read?

items.hook: The city of La Paz, in what is today Bolivia, declares its independence from the Spanish Crown during the La Paz revolution and forms the Junta Tuitiva, the first independent government in Spanish America, led by Pedro Domingo Murillo.
items.body: La Paz, officially Nuestra Señora de La Paz is the seat of government of Bolivia. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Three Roman legates place - acting illegally on their own authority - a bull of excommunication against Michael I Cerularius, the patriarch of Constantinople, and his followers on the altar of Hagia Sophia during Saturday afternoon divine liturgy. Historians frequently describe the event as the formal start of the East–West Schism.
items.body: A papal legate or apostolic legate is a personal representative of the Pope to foreign nations, to some other part of the Catholic Church, or to representatives of a state or monarchy. One of those things you can't un-know.
